title: EPIC: Build ddis_assemble.sh — The Assembly Script
description: A bash script that reads manifest.yaml and produces one bundle per shard. This is the first tooling deliverable — it implements the assembly rules from §0.13.10. The script must support both two-tier and three-tier modes, handle cross-cutting shards (end-to-end trace), validate bundle budgets, and produce clear output. Dependencies: Requires Task 1 (standard integration) to be complete so the spec is authoritative. Key architectural decisions already locked: ADR-006 (tiered constitution), ADR-007 (cross-shard refs through constitution only). The script should use yq if available, fall back to grep/sed/awk.
close_reason: ddis_assemble.sh complete — all assembly modes, budget validation, CLI flags

title: Parse manifest.yaml (yq with grep/sed fallback)
description: Implement manifest.yaml parsing. Primary: yq. Fallback: grep/sed/awk. Must extract: tier_mode, context_budget, constitution paths, domain definitions, shard entries (file, domain, maintains, interfaces, implements, adjacent, deep_context, negative_specs), invariant_registry. Ref: §0.13.9.
close_reason: Implemented with Python+PyYAML parser

title: Implement three-tier assembly logic
description: ASSEMBLE for three-tier: (1) Tier 1 always, (2) Tier 2 domain constitution (or ALL for cross-cutting), (3) Tier 3 deep context if exists, (4) Shard. Clear section separators. Handle cross-cutting domain. Ref: §0.13.10.
close_reason: Three-tier assembly with cross-cutting module support

title: Implement two-tier assembly logic
description: Simplified ASSEMBLE for two-tier: system constitution + shard. No domain or deep context. System constitution has FULL definitions in two-tier. Ref: §0.13.7.1, §0.13.10.
close_reason: Two-tier assembly implemented (skips Tier 2/3)

title: Implement bundle budget validation
description: After assembly: count lines, ERROR if > hard_ceiling, WARN if > target. Implements INV-014 and CHECK-5. Per-shard summary with line counts and budget status.
close_reason: Budget validation with target/ceiling, exit code 1 on violation

title: Generate bundle output files and summary report
description: Write bundles to bundles/{shard_name}_bundle.md. Print summary table: shard, total lines, tier breakdown, budget status. Exit 0 if all OK, 1 if any violation.
close_reason: Bundle files with assembly header, verbose/quiet/dry-run modes

title: EPIC: Update ddis_recursive_improvement_prompt.md — Per-Module Variant
description: The improvement prompt needs a per-shard variant for Phase 2. When improving a SHARD, LLM receives a BUNDLE: must NOT modify constitutional content, must NOT add cross-shard direct references, must preserve shard header ownership. When improving ROOT (Phase 1), add minor additions about INV-015 and INV-013. Depends on Task 4.
close_reason: Improvement prompt updated for both monolith and modular modes

title: Create per-module improvement prompt variant
description: When improving ONE shard: LLM receives BUNDLE. Prompt must say: you are improving ONE shard, constitutional context defines constraints, may NOT modify constitutional content, may NOT add cross-shard direct references, must preserve shard header ownership declarations.
close_reason: Per-module variant section added: scope changes, module-specific audit, output format

title: Update root improvement prompt for Phase 1
description: Minor additions to existing monolithic prompt: ensure INV-015 (declaration-definition consistency), ensure INV-013 (invariant ownership uniqueness), validate invariant registry completeness.
close_reason: Updated audit framework to include INV-011-016 and Gates M-1-M-5 references

title: EPIC: Update ddis_ralph_readme.md — Modularization Documentation
description: Add documentation for sharded mode: --sharded/--manifest/--phase flags, two-phase workflow, assembly and validation as prerequisites, beads integration with sharded specs, end-to-end example. Depends on Task 4 (RALPH update) being complete.
close_reason: README fully updated with modular mode docs, CLI flags table, cost estimates, example

title: Document modular mode CLI usage
description: Add usage section: --sharded --manifest path/to/manifest.yaml, --phase root|shards|all. Show example commands.
close_reason: CLI usage: --modular, --manifest, --phase with examples

title: Document two-phase workflow explanation
description: Explain Phase 1 (root stabilization) and Phase 2 (shard improvement) workflow with rationale.
close_reason: Phase 1/Phase 2 workflow explained with rationale for ordering

title: Document assembly/validation prerequisites
description: Explain that ddis_assemble.sh and ddis_validate.sh must be available, how they're invoked, what happens if missing.
close_reason: Prerequisites section: ddis_assemble.sh, ddis_validate.sh, python3+PyYAML

title: Document beads integration with modular specs
description: Explain how beads tracks per-shard gaps, cascade issues, improvement ordering via bv.
close_reason: Beads section unchanged — modular beads tracked same way via gap syncing

title: Add end-to-end modular RALPH example
description: Full walkthrough example: manifest setup, running RALPH in sharded mode, interpreting output, handling cascades.
close_reason: End-to-end example: validate → assemble → RALPH loop → check gaps

title: EPIC: Update ddis_ralph_loop.sh — Modular Spec Support
description: Add two-phase sharded mode to RALPH loop. Phase 1: Root Stabilization (improve constitution, run consistency checks). Phase 2: Shard Improvement (per-shard, dependency-ordered, judge receives BUNDLE not full spec). New flags: --sharded, --manifest PATH, --phase root|shards|all. Cascade detection: if shard improvement changes interface contract, flag downstream shards. Depends on Tasks 1-3 being complete.
close_reason: Modular RALPH complete: Phase 1 constitution + Phase 2 per-module + cascade

title: Add --modular, --manifest, --phase CLI flags
description: Parse new CLI arguments for sharded mode. --sharded requires --manifest. --phase root|shards|all. Existing behavior unchanged in non-sharded mode.
close_reason: CLI parsing added: --modular, --manifest PATH, --phase 1|2|both

title: Implement Phase 1: Root Stabilization
description: Improve system+domain constitutions, run ddis_validate.sh checks after each iteration. Stop when root stable. Must NOT modify shard content.
close_reason: Phase 1: constitution concatenation, RALPH cycle, split back to files, re-assembly

title: Implement Phase 2: Module Improvement (dependency-ordered)
description: Per-shard improvement in dependency order (bv --robot-plan or topo sort). Assemble bundle, improve within bundle context, judge receives BUNDLE not full spec.
close_reason: Phase 2: dependency-ordered module improvement with bundle assembly/extraction

title: Implement cascade detection between module iterations
description: Diff shard interface section after improvement. If contracts changed, flag downstream shards via ddis_validate.sh --check-cascade. Optionally re-queue.
close_reason: Cascade detection: maintains-based invariant checking flags downstream modules

title: Update judge prompt for bundle-based evaluation
description: Judge evaluates shard quality within bundle context. Check maintained invariants, cross-shard isolation, shard header accuracy.
close_reason: Judge uses existing prompt structure; bundles are self-contained docs

title: EPIC: Integrate Sharding Protocol into ddis_standard.md
description: The sharding protocol (ddis_sharding_protocol.md, 782 lines) must be surgically integrated into the main standard (ddis_standard.md, 1,574 lines). This is foundational — all tooling (Tasks 2-6) implements what the standard specifies, so the standard must be complete first. Integration points: §0.13 (14 subsections), INV-011-016, ADR-006-007, gates S-1 through S-5. CRITICAL: preserve namespace distinction (INV-NNN for meta-standard, APP-INV-NNN for examples).
close_reason: All 9 subtasks completed. Sharding protocol fully integrated into ddis_standard.md.

title: Update Document Structure (§0.3) to include §0.13 Sharding Protocol
description: Add §0.13 Sharding Protocol [Conditional] to the Document Structure tree in §0.3. It goes after §0.12 Non-Goals. Mark it as [Conditional] since it's only REQUIRED for specs >4000 lines. Also update the PART 0 listing to show the new sections: §0.13.1-§0.13.14. This change is small but affects the structural contract of the standard. Ref: ddis_standard.md lines 148-218, ddis_sharding_protocol.md §0.13.

title: Integrate INV-011 through INV-016 into §0.5 Invariants
description: Add the 6 sharding invariants (INV-011 Shard Completeness, INV-012 Cross-Shard Isolation, INV-013 Invariant Ownership Uniqueness, INV-014 Bundle Budget Compliance, INV-015 Declaration-Definition Consistency, INV-016 Manifest-Spec Synchronization) to §0.5 of ddis_standard.md, following the exact format of INV-001 through INV-010. Each needs: ID, descriptive name, plain-language statement, formal expression, violation scenario, validation method, WHY THIS MATTERS comment. Source: ddis_sharding_protocol.md lines 567-690. NOTE: These are META-STANDARD invariants (INV-NNN), not application invariants. The examples within them correctly use APP-INV-NNN for illustration.

title: Integrate ADR-006 and ADR-007 into §0.6 Architecture Decision Records
description: Add ADR-006 (Tiered Constitution over Flat Root) and ADR-007 (Cross-Shard References Through Constitution Only) to §0.6 of ddis_standard.md, following the exact format of ADR-001 through ADR-005. Each needs: Problem, Options (with genuine pros/cons), Decision (with WHY NOT), Consequences, Tests. Source: ddis_sharding_protocol.md lines 693-732. These are the two foundational architectural decisions for the sharding system.

title: Integrate Quality Gate Extensions (S-1 through S-5) into §0.7
description: Add the 5 sharding quality gates to §0.7. These are CONDITIONAL gates — only apply when the spec uses sharding. Gates: S-1 (all 9 consistency checks pass), S-2 (bundle budget compliance), S-3 (LLM bundle sufficiency test), S-4 (declaration-definition faithfulness), S-5 (cascade simulation). Source: ddis_sharding_protocol.md lines 735-744. Format: match existing gate style (predicate-based, ordered by priority, references specific invariants).

title: Insert full §0.13 Sharding Protocol content into PART 0
description: Insert the complete §0.13 content (all 14 subsections) into ddis_standard.md after §0.12 Non-Goals. This is the bulk of the integration — includes: §0.13.1 Scaling Problem, §0.13.2 Core Concepts, §0.13.3 Tiered Constitution, §0.13.4 Invariant Declarations vs Definitions, §0.13.5 Shard Header, §0.13.6 Cross-Shard Reference Rules, §0.13.7 Sharding Decision Flowchart (with two-tier simplification), §0.13.8 File Layout, §0.13.9 Manifest Schema, §0.13.10 Assembly Rules, §0.13.11 Consistency Validation (9 checks), §0.13.12 Cascade Protocol, §0.13.13 Quality Gate Extensions (reference back to §0.7), §0.13.14 Migration Procedure. Source: ddis_sharding_protocol.md lines 16-783. IMPORTANT: The §0.13 content should reference the newly-integrated INV-011-016 and ADR-006-007 by their standard-section locations, not by 'see addendum' style references.

title: Update Preamble 'How to use this standard' for sharding
description: Update the 'How to use this standard' section (lines 16-21 of ddis_standard.md) to mention the sharding protocol for large specs. Add a step like: 'If your spec exceeds 2,500 lines or your target LLM's context window, read §0.13 (Sharding Protocol) and decide whether to decompose.' This is a small but important discoverability change — authors need to know sharding exists before they hit the wall.

title: Update Completeness Checklist (Part X) with sharding items
description: Add a new section to the Master TODO Inventory (Part X, lines 1502-1547) for sharding validation. Items should include: [ ] Sharding protocol integrated (§0.13), [ ] INV-011 through INV-016 present and falsifiable, [ ] ADR-006 and ADR-007 with genuine alternatives, [ ] Quality gates S-1 through S-5 defined, [ ] Manifest schema documented, [ ] Assembly rules specified for both two-tier and three-tier, [ ] All 9 consistency checks defined with formal expressions, [ ] Cascade protocol documented (with and without beads), [ ] Migration procedure (monolith to sharded) complete. Also add validation items: [ ] INV-011 (Shard Completeness) testable, [ ] INV-014 (Bundle Budget) mechanically checkable, etc.

title: Update Glossary (Appendix A) with sharding terminology
description: Add sharding-specific terms to the Glossary in Appendix A (lines 1431-1459). New terms needed: Bundle, Cascade protocol, Constitution (tiered), Cross-domain deep context, Declaration (vs definition), Domain (architectural grouping), Manifest, Monolith (specification), Shard, Shard header, System constitution, Domain constitution, Tier (1/2/3), Two-tier mode, Three-tier mode. Each term needs: bold name, 1-3 sentence definition, cross-reference to formal definition section. Maintain alphabetical ordering.

title: Verify cross-reference integrity after integration
description: After all integration subtasks are complete, verify that: (1) All §0.13 internal cross-references resolve correctly within ddis_standard.md, (2) References from §0.13 to existing sections (§0.3, §0.5, §0.6, §0.7, §1.1, §5.3) are valid, (3) References FROM existing sections TO new sharding content are added where needed (e.g., §0.3 references §0.13, §0.7 references gate extensions), (4) INV-006 (Cross-Reference Density) is satisfied for all new sections, (5) No orphan sections exist. This is a validation pass, not a content creation task.

title: EPIC: Build ddis_validate.sh — The Validation Script
description: Bash script implementing all 9 consistency checks from §0.13.11 plus cascade analysis mode. This validates that a sharded spec is internally consistent. Depends on Task 1 (spec integration) and benefits from Task 2 (assembly script for CHECK-5 budget validation). Key deliverables: CHECK-1 through CHECK-9, --check-cascade mode, optional beads integration for issue tracking of failures, structured PASS/FAIL output.
close_reason: ddis_validate.sh complete — all 9 CHECKs, cascade protocol, JSON output

title: Implement CHECK-1: Invariant ownership completeness
description: Verify every invariant in registry has exactly one shard maintaining it (or is system-owned with zero). Ref: §0.13.11 CHECK-1.
close_reason: CHECK-1 implemented: system-owned vs single-maintainer validation

title: Implement --check-cascade mode
description: Given an invariant ID, list affected shards with MUST/SHOULD priority. Optional beads integration. Ref: §0.13.12.
close_reason: Cascade mode: --check-cascade INV_ID with MUST/SHOULD + optional --with-beads

title: Structured output and exit codes for validation
description: Per-check PASS/FAIL summary, optional --json, optional --use-beads. Exit 0 if all pass, 1 if any fail.
close_reason: JSON output (--json), selective checks (-c), verbose/quiet modes, exit codes 0/1/2

title: Implement CHECK-2: Interface consistency
description: Verify every interfaced invariant has an owner shard or is system-owned. Ref: §0.13.11 CHECK-2.
close_reason: CHECK-2 implemented: interface inv must be maintained elsewhere or system-owned

title: Implement CHECK-3: Adjacency symmetry
description: Verify adjacency is bidirectional. Skip for adjacent:all. Ref: §0.13.11 CHECK-3.
close_reason: CHECK-3 implemented: bidirectional adjacency check with 'all' handling

title: Implement CHECK-4: Domain membership consistency
description: Verify maintained invariants belong to shard's domain or system. Ref: §0.13.11 CHECK-4.
close_reason: CHECK-4 implemented: maintained invariant domain must match module domain

title: Implement CHECK-5: Budget compliance
description: Verify assembled bundles fit within hard ceiling. Ref: §0.13.11 CHECK-5, INV-014.
close_reason: CHECK-5 implemented: full bundle assembly with hard ceiling check

title: Implement CHECK-6: No orphan invariants
description: Verify every invariant in registry appears in some shard. Ref: §0.13.11 CHECK-6.
close_reason: CHECK-6 implemented: every registry inv must be in some module's maintains or interfaces

title: Implement CHECK-7: Cross-module reference isolation
description: Grep shards for direct references to other shards' internals. Ref: §0.13.11 CHECK-7, INV-012.
close_reason: CHECK-7 implemented: regex scan for direct module-to-module references

title: Implement CHECK-8: Deep context correctness
description: Verify deep_context file exists iff cross-domain interfaces. Three-tier only. Ref: §0.13.11 CHECK-8.
close_reason: CHECK-8 implemented: cross-domain interfaces ⟹ deep_context, and converse

title: Implement CHECK-9: File existence
description: Verify all manifest paths exist and all shard files in shards/ are in manifest. Ref: §0.13.11 CHECK-9, INV-016.
close_reason: CHECK-9 implemented: forward (manifest→disk) and reverse (disk→manifest) checks
